CONTENT.Club career: Danielsen started with KÍ Klaksvík, playing his first game in their 2000 league season. After he became a regular in the national team he was loaned to Norwegian side Sogndal before he signed for Danish outfit Boldklubben Frem. In the wintertransfer 2010, he signed FC Roskilde.
CONTENT.External links: Profile at faroesoccer.com (in Faroese)Profile at national-football-teams.comBoldklubben Frem profile (in Danish)
CONTENT.International career: Danielsen is a regular in the Faroese national team. He made his debut in April 2003 in friendly match against Kazakhstan, coming on as a substitute for Hans Fróði Hansen.

SUMMARY: Atli Danielsen (born 15 August 1983) is a Faroese footballer who currently plays for KÍ Klaksvík in the Premier League of the Faroe Islands.
